response.justification: A seasoned, versatile engineer with ~15 years of experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, plus strong fundamentals in testing, performance, and architecture. However, the role centers on hands-on C++/STL development for industrial automation, and the candidate's C++ exposure is thin and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis), with the last decade focused on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers in the required sense, IoT/industrial protocols, or IEC 62443 / secure development lifecycle experience. The low-latency DLang work and craftsmanship background are transferable, but the core technical and domain alignment is weak.
strengths: Extensive multi-domain engineering experience with strong TDD, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship practices
Proven low-latency, high-throughput, concurrent systems work (DLang microservices at Sociomantic)
Demonstrated mentoring and communication through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal and over a decade old; not a recent core competency
No industrial automation, IoT, MQTT, or protocol (Modbus/OPC UA/IEC 61850) background
No evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) or IEC 62443 cybersecurity experience

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation / IoT / protocol domain fit
